channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) has officially launched its fifth international research funding call titled “Preparing for Tomorrow – Societies and Strategies in Times of Transition.” The initiative seeks to support innovative and collaborative research aimed at helping societies understand, anticipate, and respond to future challenges in an increasingly uncertain world. As the world grapples with […]

The post Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP) has officially launched its fifth international research funding call titled “Preparing for Tomorrow – Societies and Strategies in Times of Transition.” The initiative seeks to support innovative and collaborative research aimed at helping societies understand, anticipate, and respond to future challenges in an increasingly uncertain world.

As the world grapples with climate change, pandemics, geopolitical tensions, technological disruptions, economic instability, and emerging social challenges, there is a growing need for evidence-based strategies that can help communities and policymakers prepare for the future. Through this funding call, T-AP aims to strengthen international cooperation among researchers and promote interdisciplinary solutions to complex global issues.

About the Trans-Atlantic Platform (T-AP)

The Trans-Atlantic Platform for Social Sciences and Humanities is a large-scale collaboration involving research funding agencies from Europe, North America, South America, and Africa. The platform promotes international dialogue and collaborative research to address common societal challenges through social sciences and humanities research.

The Preparing for Tomorrow initiative represents the fifth joint call organized by T-AP and focuses on building resilient and inclusive societies capable of navigating uncertain futures.

Aim of the Grant

The program will support collaborative research projects that advance understanding of how societies:

  • Envision future scenarios.
  • Prepare for emerging crises.
  • Respond to uncertainty.
  • Develop strategies for resilience and sustainable development.
  • Inform policy and decision-making processes at local, national, and international levels.

Projects are expected to generate knowledge that contributes to forward-looking and inclusive responses to societal transitions.

Research Themes

Applicants are invited to address one or more of the following themes:

1. Understanding Uncertainty

Research may examine:

  • Sources of uncertainty.
  • Economic and social costs.
  • Communication of risks.
  • Strategies for reducing uncertainty.

2. Futures and Crises

Researchers may explore:

  • Historical perspectives on crises.
  • Cultural interpretations of the future.
  • Regional differences in preparing for change.
  • Social responses to emerging threats.

3. Response Strategies and Coordination

Projects may investigate:

  • Governance systems.
  • Coordination mechanisms.
  • Public policy responses.
  • Institutional preparedness.

4. Prevention and Preparation

Research may focus on:

  • Ethical and normative questions.
  • Long-term preparedness.
  • Prevention frameworks.
  • Responsibility and accountability in crisis management.

Key Features of the Grant

The program aims to:

  • Support international and interdisciplinary research collaboration.
  • Promote engagement with communities and policymakers.
  • Encourage participation and development of early-career researchers.
  • Foster diversity within research teams.
  • Address opportunities and risks associated with technological, political, environmental, and societal change.

Eligible Disciplines

The call welcomes proposals employing:

  • Qualitative research methods.
  • Quantitative approaches.
  • Mixed-methods research.
  • Historical studies.
  • Future-oriented methodologies.
  • Interdisciplinary approaches within Social Sciences and Humanities.

Consortium Requirements

Applications must involve:

  • At least three eligible principal investigators.
  • Participants from at least three different participating countries.
  • Representation from both sides of the Atlantic.
  • Compliance with national funding regulations.

Applicants are encouraged to consult the national addenda provided by their funding organizations to confirm eligibility.

Duration of Projects

Funded projects are expected to run for:

  • Between 24 and 36 months.

Benefits of the Preparing for Tomorrow Grant

Successful applicants will gain:

  • Access to international research funding.
  • Opportunities for transnational collaboration.
  • Engagement with policymakers and stakeholders.
  • Increased visibility of research outputs.
  • Capacity-building opportunities.
  • Support for interdisciplinary innovation.
  • Opportunities for early-career researchers to develop internationally.

Important Dates

Call Launch

17 April 2026

Letter of Intent Deadline

8 July 2026

Full Application Deadline

28 October 2026

Evaluation Period

March – April 2027

Funding Decisions Announced

April – May 2027

Expected Start of Awards

July – September 2027

How to Apply

Applications are submitted through the German Research Foundation (DFG) Elan Portal by the Lead Principal Investigator on behalf of the consortium.

Researchers should:

  1. Build an international consortium.
  2. Review the call scope and national addenda.
  3. Submit the Letter of Intent before 8 July 2026.
  4. Prepare and submit the full proposal by 28 October 2026.
  5. Ensure compliance with requirements established by their national funding agencies.

Applicants can access the call guidelines and application documents here

The Preparing for Tomorrow Grant represents a significant opportunity for social sciences and humanities researchers seeking to contribute to global preparedness and resilience. Through international collaboration and interdisciplinary approaches, the program seeks to generate new knowledge and strategies that will help societies navigate uncertainty and build a more sustainable future.

Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here

 

The post Preparing for Tomorrow 2026 Grant: International Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OPCW Scholarships 2026 for the Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) in The Hague, Netherlands
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Professionals working in arms control, non-proliferation, international security, diplomacy, and chemical weapons policy now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their expertise through one of the world’s most prestigious disarmament training programmes. Applications are now open for the 2026 Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD), jointly organized by […]

The post OPCW Scholarships 2026 for the Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) in The Hague, Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals working in arms control, non-proliferation, international security, diplomacy, and chemical weapons policy now have an exceptional opportunity to strengthen their expertise through one of the world’s most prestigious disarmament training programmes.

Applications are now open for the 2026 Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD), jointly organized by the Asser Institute and the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) in The Hague, Netherlands.

As part of this initiative, the OPCW is offering tuition fee waiver scholarships funded by the European Union for selected participants working in civil society, NGOs, think tanks, and academia.

The intensive five-day programme will take place from 26 October to 30 October 2026 and provides participants with advanced practical and policy-oriented training on the rapidly evolving global challenges surrounding weapons of mass destruction, arms control, export regulations, emerging technologies, and international law.

Programme Overview

The international arms control landscape is currently facing unprecedented pressure due to geopolitical fragmentation, technological disruption, and the weakening of global non-proliferation frameworks.

Concerns over nuclear weapons have returned to global political discourse, chemical weapons continue to pose major threats in active conflicts, and emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and biotechnology are reshaping traditional security environments faster than existing legal systems can adapt.

Against this backdrop, the Asser–OPCW Training Programme was designed to equip professionals with the legal, diplomatic, technical, and strategic knowledge required to navigate today’s complex disarmament and non-proliferation challenges.

Participants will engage directly with leading international experts, practitioners, policymakers, and researchers through interactive lectures, discussions, case studies, and field visits.

Scholarship Benefits

Selected scholarship recipients will receive:

  • Full tuition fee waiver worth €1,895
  • Access to expert-led sessions and workshops
  • Networking opportunities with international security professionals
  • Exposure to high-level practitioners in arms control and disarmament
  • Participation in exclusive institutional field visits
  • Advanced practical understanding of modern WMD challenges
  • Professional development in international law, export controls, and security governance

The scholarships are funded by the European Union and specifically support professionals from civil society organizations and academic sectors.

Female applicants and candidates from lower-income countries are strongly encouraged to apply.

Programme Dates and Location

  • Dates: 26 October – 30 October 2026
  • Duration: Five days
  • Venue: Asser Institute and OPCW
  • Location: The Hague, Netherlands

Address:
R.J. Schimmelpennincklaan 20-22
2517 JN The Hague
Netherlands

Key Topics Covered During the Programme

Participants will receive comprehensive training on some of the world’s most urgent disarmament and security challenges.

1. Nuclear Weapons and Strategic Stability

Sessions will examine:

  • Expanding nuclear stockpiles
  • Declining arms control agreements
  • Challenges facing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT)
  • Deadlocked NPT Review Conferences
  • Future pathways for nuclear disarmament

2. Chemical Weapons and Accountability

Participants will explore:

  • Use of chemical weapons in modern conflicts
  • International accountability mechanisms
  • Verification and compliance systems
  • Challenges of misinformation and disinformation
  • Enforcement of chemical weapons conventions

3. Biological Weapons and Synthetic Biology

Key discussions include:

  • Risks posed by synthetic biology
  • Advances in pathogen engineering
  • Weaknesses in the Biological Weapons Convention
  • Biosecurity governance challenges
  • Future biological weapons threats

4. Export Controls and Emerging Technologies

The programme also addresses rapidly developing technologies, including:

  • Artificial intelligence-enabled systems
  • Autonomous weapons
  • Hypersonic missile technology
  • Export control regulations
  • Legal and ethical gaps in emerging military technologies

Exclusive Field Visits

One of the most valuable components of the programme is direct institutional exposure through guided visits to internationally significant facilities.

Participants will visit:

OPCW Centre for Chemistry and Technology (ChemTech)

This advanced facility supports scientific research, verification training, and international cooperation related to chemical weapons prohibition.

OPCW Headquarters

Participants will gain firsthand insight into the operations of one of the world’s leading international organizations dedicated to eliminating chemical weapons globally.

Confirmed International Experts and Speakers

The programme brings together highly respected scholars and practitioners from globally recognized institutions.

Confirmed speakers include:

  • Dr León Castellanos-Jankiewicz – Asser Institute
  • Simon Cleobury – Geneva Centre for Security Policy
  • Dr Sico van der Meer – Clingendael Institute
  • Dr Oliver Meier – European Leadership Network
  • Ionut Suseanu – International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA)
  • Dr Ana Sánchez-Cobaleda – University of Barcelona
  • Dr Sibylle Bauer – Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI)
  • Dr James Revill – United Nations Institute for Disarmament Research (UNIDIR)
  • Dr Miranda Smith – SIPRI
  • Dr Edward Howell – University of Oxford
  • Dr Abdolrasool Farzam Divsallar – UNIDIR

Who Should Apply?

This programme is ideal for professionals and students seeking specialized expertise in international security and disarmament policy.

Suitable applicants include:

  • Government officials
  • Diplomats
  • NGO professionals
  • Think tank researchers
  • Academic scholars
  • International organization staff
  • Compliance officers
  • Legal advisers
  • Graduate students transitioning into the security field

Applicants with backgrounds in international relations, law, diplomacy, political science, security studies, public policy, arms control, and strategic affairs are especially encouraged to apply.

Why This Programme Matters

The global security environment is evolving rapidly. Traditional arms control systems are increasingly challenged by geopolitical tensions, technological innovation, and the growing complexity of modern warfare.

This programme provides participants with:

  • Practical policy knowledge
  • Exposure to real-world disarmament challenges
  • International networking opportunities
  • Institutional access to global security actors
  • Specialized expertise highly valued in diplomacy and international organizations

For professionals seeking careers in international security, arms control, diplomacy, compliance, or multilateral policy-making, this programme represents a significant career-development opportunity.

Tuition Fee

  • Standard Programme Fee: €1,895
  • Scholarship Support: Tuition fee waivers available through OPCW scholarships

Application Deadline

1 August 2026

Learn More & Apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the OPCW Scholarships 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post OPCW Scholarships 2026 for the Asser–OPCW Training Programme on Disarmament and Non-Proliferation of Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) in The Hague, Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Embassy of Denmark in Ghana Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Communication and Cultural Officer Position in Accra
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana has announced an exciting career opportunity for talented communication professionals seeking to contribute to international relations, cultural diplomacy, and public engagement. Applications are now open for the position of Communication and Cultural Officer, a role designed for highly motivated individuals with strong communication skills, creativity, and a passion for […]

The post Embassy of Denmark in Ghana Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Communication and Cultural Officer Position in Accra appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana has announced an exciting career opportunity for talented communication professionals seeking to contribute to international relations, cultural diplomacy, and public engagement. Applications are now open for the position of Communication and Cultural Officer, a role designed for highly motivated individuals with strong communication skills, creativity, and a passion for strengthening international partnerships.

Based in Accra, Ghana, the successful candidate will play a central role in enhancing Denmark’s visibility, public diplomacy efforts, and cultural engagement across Ghana and several side-accredited countries covered by the Embassy.

If you have experience in communications, event management, journalism, public relations, cultural programming, or international affairs, this position offers an excellent opportunity to work within a respected diplomatic mission while contributing to impactful cultural and communication initiatives.

About the Embassy of Denmark in Ghana

The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana is part of the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs and serves as Denmark’s official diplomatic representation in Ghana. In addition to Ghana, the Embassy also covers several side-accredited countries, including:

  • Guinea
  • Ivory Coast
  • Liberia
  • Sierra Leone
  • São Tomé and Príncipe
  • Togo

The Embassy works to strengthen diplomatic relations, promote Danish interests, support development cooperation, and foster cultural exchange between Denmark and countries within its area of responsibility.

Position Overview

Job Title

Communication and Cultural Officer

Employment Type

  • Full-time contract (40 hours per week), or
  • Part-time contract under local terms and conditions

Location

67 Dr. Isert Street, North Ridge, Accra, Ghana

Start Date

As soon as possible

Application Deadline

Wednesday, 8 July 2026

Role Summary

The Communication and Cultural Officer will be responsible for ensuring effective and continuous communication on behalf of the Embassy while strengthening Denmark’s public image and cultural presence in Ghana and neighboring countries under the Embassy’s accreditation.

The role combines strategic communication, public diplomacy, stakeholder engagement, event coordination, and cultural project management. The selected candidate will work closely with Embassy staff, media professionals, cultural stakeholders, and international partners to showcase Danish priorities, initiatives, and partnerships.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will undertake a variety of communication and cultural diplomacy functions, including:

1. Lead Cultural Programming and Events

The officer will plan, coordinate, and execute a range of cultural and diplomatic activities, including:

  • High-level diplomatic events
  • Official visits
  • Seminars and conferences
  • Networking engagements
  • Cultural exhibitions
  • Public diplomacy initiatives

These activities will help strengthen Denmark’s profile and visibility in Ghana and other countries covered by the Embassy.

2. Develop Communication Content

The role involves creating compelling communication materials that effectively communicate Embassy priorities and activities.

Examples include:

  • Articles
  • Speeches
  • Press releases
  • Presentations
  • Visual communication materials
  • Public information content

The selected candidate must be capable of adapting messages to different audiences and communication channels.

3. Manage Digital Communication Platforms

The Communication and Cultural Officer will oversee the Embassy’s communication channels and ensure a consistent online presence.

Responsibilities include:

  • Managing social media accounts
  • Updating website content
  • Supporting digital campaigns
  • Monitoring online engagement
  • Producing content for digital audiences

4. Build Strategic Media Relationships

A key aspect of the role is developing and maintaining professional relationships with:

  • Journalists
  • Media organizations
  • Communication professionals
  • Cultural attachés
  • International organizations
  • Diplomatic missions

Strong stakeholder engagement will support the Embassy’s communication objectives and public diplomacy efforts.

5. Coordinate Cultural Projects

The successful candidate will also oversee cultural initiatives related to:

  • Music
  • Literature
  • Performing arts
  • Exhibitions
  • Creative industries
  • Cultural exchange programs

These projects will help deepen cultural connections and strengthen bilateral relationships.

Required Qualifications

Applicants must possess a strong academic background and relevant professional experience.

Minimum requirements include:

  • A Bachelor’s degree or equivalent qualification in a relevant discipline such as:
    • Communications
    • Journalism
    • Event Management
    • Public Relations
    • Media Studies
    • Related fields
  • Demonstrated experience managing events and communication campaigns.
  • Excellent command of written and spoken English.

Preferred Skills and Competencies

The Embassy is looking for a candidate who can demonstrate:

Strong Communication Skills

Applicants should possess excellent verbal and written communication abilities and be able to communicate effectively with diverse audiences.

Storytelling Ability

The successful candidate should be capable of creating engaging narratives that highlight diplomatic, cultural, and development initiatives.

Collaboration and Teamwork

The position requires working closely with colleagues, stakeholders, media representatives, and external partners.

Creativity and Innovation

Creative thinking and the ability to develop fresh communication and cultural engagement ideas are highly valued.

Cultural Awareness

Candidates should demonstrate sensitivity to cultural diversity and an appreciation for international cooperation.

Professional Growth Mindset

The Embassy seeks individuals who are eager to learn, share knowledge, and continuously improve their professional skills.

Interest in International Affairs

A strong interest in diplomacy, international relations, development cooperation, and global affairs will be an advantage.

Additional Advantages

While not mandatory, the following qualifications will strengthen an application:

  • Proficiency in French
  • Previous experience working at:
    • Embassies
    • International organizations
    • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
    • Multicultural institutions

Employment Benefits

The Embassy offers an attractive employment package under local terms and conditions.

Benefits include:

Flexible Employment Options

Applicants may be considered for:

  • Full-time employment
  • Part-time employment

Annual Leave

Employees receive:

  • Five weeks of paid annual leave

Flexible Work Arrangements

The Embassy offers a flexible work scheme to support work-life balance.

Health Insurance Coverage

The successful candidate and eligible dependent family members will receive health insurance that includes coverage for medical expenses.

Competitive Salary

Salary will be determined according to:

  • Qualifications
  • Relevant professional experience
  • Demonstrated work performance
  • Embassy salary scale guidelines

Probation Period

The employment contract includes a mutual probation period of three months.

Security Clearance Requirement

Employment is subject to security clearance by Danish authorities.

Applicants should be prepared to provide:

  • A valid No Criminal Record certificate

Failure to obtain the required security clearance may affect employment eligibility.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must submit their applications in English.

Application packages should include:

  1. Brief cover letter
  2. One-page Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Educational qualification documents
  4. Relevant recommendations
  5. Contact details of one or two references

Submission Instructions

Applications should be sent by email with the subject line:

“Communication and Cultural Officer”

All documents must be submitted in PDF format, either:

  • As separate PDF attachments, or
  • Combined into a single PDF document

Application Deadline

8 July 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Recruitment Process

Following the application review process:

  • Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews.
  • Interviews are expected to take place within two weeks after the application deadline.
  • Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Equal Opportunity Commitment

The Embassy of Denmark in Ghana is committed to equal employment opportunities and welcomes applications from all qualified individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Sex
  • Religion
  • Age
  • Disability status

The Embassy values diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from candidates of varied backgrounds.

Data Protection and Privacy

The Embassy processes job applications in accordance with the European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

By submitting an application, candidates consent to the processing of their personal data for recruitment purposes. Applicants are encouraged to review the Danish Ministry of Foreign Affairs privacy notice before applying to understand:

  • Data processing procedures
  • Applicant rights
  • Consent withdrawal options
  • Complaint procedures regarding personal data handling

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The Communication and Cultural Officer position offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of diplomacy, communications, culture, and international engagement. Successful candidates will contribute directly to strengthening Denmark’s visibility and partnerships across West Africa while gaining valuable experience within an international diplomatic environment.

Professionals with a passion for storytelling, cultural exchange, public diplomacy, and strategic communication are strongly encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline of 8 July 2026.

Please send your application by email to accamb.recruitment@um.dk, with “Communication and

Cultural Officer” in the subject line.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Embassy of Denmark in Ghana Recruitment 2026: Apply for the Communication and Cultural Officer Position in Accra appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026–2027 in the United States
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The University of Maryland School of Pharmacy is inviting applications for its highly regarded Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship within the Department of Pharmacy Practice, Sciences, and Health Outcomes Research (P-SHOR). This fellowship is hosted through the renowned PATIENTS Program at the University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMB), offering scholars a unique opportunity to gain advanced multidisciplinary […]

The post University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026–2027 in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Maryland School of Pharmacy is inviting applications for its highly regarded Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship within the Department of Pharmacy Practice, Sciences, and Health Outcomes Research (P-SHOR). This fellowship is hosted through the renowned PATIENTS Program at the University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMB), offering scholars a unique opportunity to gain advanced multidisciplinary training in community-engaged research while working alongside leading researchers, community partners, healthcare stakeholders, and funding agencies.

This postdoctoral fellowship is designed for individuals who have completed a PhD or equivalent doctoral degree and are interested in advancing their careers in academia, government, pharmaceutical industries, healthcare research, and consulting. Fellows will receive extensive mentorship, hands-on research experience, grant-writing support, and opportunities to contribute to impactful projects funded by major organizations such as the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ), the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the National Institutes of Health (NIH), the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI), pharmaceutical companies, and private foundations.

Located in Baltimore, Maryland, this fellowship provides a dynamic environment where fellows can develop expertise in community-engaged research while contributing to meaningful initiatives that improve healthcare outcomes and strengthen community partnerships.

About the Community-Engaged Research Fellowship

The Community-Engaged Research (CEnR) Postdoctoral Fellowship is part of the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy’s commitment to advancing patient-centered and community-focused healthcare research.

The fellowship offers structured and mentored training experiences that prepare fellows for leadership positions across multiple sectors. Participants will work closely with experienced investigators and community organizations while gaining practical exposure to all phases of the research process.

The fellowship emphasizes:

  • Community-engaged research methodologies
  • Patient-centered outcomes research
  • Qualitative research approaches
  • Grant proposal development
  • Scientific manuscript preparation
  • Research dissemination
  • Collaborative engagement with community stakeholders
  • Academic and professional development

Fellows will become part of a vibrant research ecosystem consisting of more than 20 active projects supported by the PATIENTS Program.

Fellowship Location

The fellowship will be based at:

University of Maryland School of Pharmacy
Baltimore, Maryland, United States

The institution is widely recognized for excellence in pharmacy education, healthcare innovation, scientific research, and community engagement.

Fellowship Start Date

The fellowship offers flexibility for selected candidates.

Start Date: Flexible and negotiable, beginning anytime from Fall 2026 through Summer 2027.

This flexibility allows successful applicants to coordinate their transition into the fellowship based on their academic, professional, or personal schedules.

Fellowship Salary

Selected fellows will receive a competitive annual salary ranging from:

$63,480 – $77,076

Compensation is determined based on:

  • NIH stipend guidelines
  • Educational qualifications
  • Relevant experience
  • Professional background

Fellowship Benefits

Participants will gain access to numerous professional and academic benefits, including:

1. Advanced Research Training

Fellows receive intensive training in community-engaged and patient-centered research methodologies.

2. Mentorship from Leading Researchers

Participants will work under the guidance of distinguished investigators, including:

  • C. Daniel Mullins (Primary Investigator)
  • Claudia Baquet
  • Jasmine K. Cooper
  • Hillary Edwards
  • Michelle Medeiros

3. Grant Writing Experience

The fellowship includes practical training in developing competitive grant applications for major funding agencies and foundations.

4. Scientific Publishing Opportunities

Fellows will gain valuable experience in manuscript preparation, publication strategies, and scholarly dissemination.

5. Extensive Community Engagement

Participants will collaborate directly with community organizations, patients, healthcare professionals, and research partners.

6. Optional Coursework

Fellows may enroll in coursework related to:

  • Community-Engaged Research (CEnR)
  • Patient-Centered Outcomes Research (PCOR)

7. Career Development

The fellowship prepares participants for future careers in:

  • Universities and academic institutions
  • Government agencies
  • Pharmaceutical companies
  • Healthcare organizations
  • Research institutions
  • Consulting firms

8. Competitive Employee Benefits

Eligible fellows may access University of Maryland Baltimore postdoctoral benefits and support programs.

Research Areas and Responsibilities

Selected fellows will participate in a broad range of activities that strengthen their research and professional skills.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Conducting qualitative research studies
  • Leading focus groups and key informant interviews
  • Analyzing qualitative data
  • Engaging community stakeholders
  • Collaborating with multidisciplinary research teams
  • Preparing manuscripts for publication
  • Supporting grant proposal development
  • Contributing to ongoing community-engaged research initiatives
  • Presenting research findings

The fellowship offers extensive exposure to real-world healthcare challenges and patient-centered solutions.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Required Qualifications

  • Possess a PhD or equivalent doctoral degree
  • Demonstrate strong qualitative research skills
  • Have experience conducting focus groups
  • Have experience conducting key informant interviews
  • Demonstrate qualitative data analysis expertise
  • Possess excellent communication skills
  • Have strong organizational abilities
  • Demonstrate excellent academic writing skills
  • Possess effective time-management skills
  • Be capable of working empathetically within community settings

Preferred Qualifications

Applicants with experience in the following areas are particularly encouraged to apply:

  • Community-Engaged Research (CEnR)
  • Community-Based Participatory Research (CBPR)
  • Patient-centered research initiatives
  • Community partnership development

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following materials:

  1. Letter of Intent
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Writing Sample
  4. Three Letters of Recommendation

Incomplete applications may not receive full consideration.

Application Process

Interested applicants should submit their application through the University of Maryland’s online recruitment system using:

Taleo Requisition Number: 260000MQ

Applications are currently being reviewed.

Application Deadline

No fixed deadline has been announced.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and will continue until suitable candidates are selected. Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize their chances of consideration.

About the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy

Founded in 1841, the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy is the fourth-oldest school of pharmacy in the United States. The institution is internationally recognized for its contributions to:

  • Pharmacy education
  • Scientific discovery
  • Patient care
  • Community engagement
  • Healthcare innovation

The School’s mission is centered on improving health outcomes through research excellence, education, and meaningful community partnerships.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

This fellowship provides a rare opportunity to combine advanced qualitative research training with meaningful community engagement. Fellows benefit from direct mentorship, exposure to large-scale funded projects, publication opportunities, grant-writing experience, and collaborative research that addresses real healthcare needs.

For doctoral graduates seeking to build impactful careers in healthcare research, academia, policy, or industry, the University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship represents an exceptional professional development opportunity.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post University of Maryland School of Pharmacy Community-Engaged Research Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026–2027 in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Institute for Gender Equality Gender Equality Forum 2026 Youth Participation Call: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young People Across the European Union
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Young people across Europe now have a unique opportunity to contribute directly to important discussions shaping the future of gender equality, economic growth, and competitiveness within the European Union. The European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) has officially launched a call for youth participation in the Gender Equality Forum 2026, inviting 35 young individuals to […]

The post European Institute for Gender Equality Gender Equality Forum 2026 Youth Participation Call: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young People Across the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people across Europe now have a unique opportunity to contribute directly to important discussions shaping the future of gender equality, economic growth, and competitiveness within the European Union. The European Institute for Gender Equality (EIGE) has officially launched a call for youth participation in the Gender Equality Forum 2026, inviting 35 young individuals to attend the prestigious event in Brussels, Belgium, on 17 November 2026.

This exciting initiative is designed to ensure that youth perspectives are fully integrated into conversations about gender equality and its role in strengthening Europe’s economic and social future. Selected participants will have the opportunity to engage with policymakers, experts, employers, civil society representatives, and fellow young leaders from across Europe.

Importantly, EIGE will provide travel support for up to 30 selected participants through reimbursement of travel expenses, accommodation costs, and daily allowances, making the opportunity accessible to a diverse group of young people.

About the European Institute for Gender Equality

The European Institute for Gender Equality serves as the European Union’s knowledge centre for gender equality. As the EU agency dedicated to promoting gender equality, EIGE develops evidence-based research, tools, and resources that support policymakers in creating inclusive and transformative policies across all sectors of society.

Through initiatives such as the Gender Equality Forum, EIGE brings together stakeholders from different backgrounds to address challenges and opportunities related to equality, inclusion, and sustainable development across Europe.

What is the Gender Equality Forum 2026?

The Gender Equality Forum is one of Europe’s leading platforms for discussing pressing gender equality issues affecting the European Union. The 2026 edition arrives at a crucial moment when several major European priorities are intersecting, including:

  • Care policies and support systems
  • Ongoing discussions regarding the EU’s multiannual budget
  • Innovation and competitiveness strategies
  • Labour market inclusion
  • Economic resilience and growth

The Forum seeks to ensure that gender equality remains central to Europe’s economic and social development agenda.

Recognizing that young people are key stakeholders in Europe’s future, EIGE is placing a strong emphasis on youth participation and is actively seeking young voices to contribute ideas, experiences, and solutions.

Key Themes of the Gender Equality Forum 2026

Discussions throughout the Forum will focus on three major thematic areas:

1. Care at the Core for Resilient Economies

Participants will explore how care systems contribute to stronger economies and societies, including discussions around:

  • Care infrastructure
  • Work-life balance
  • Family support systems
  • Social protection policies
  • Economic resilience

2. Skills for Innovation: Addressing Gender Gaps in Opportunities and Workplaces

This theme will examine ways to reduce gender disparities in:

  • Education
  • Skills development
  • Employment opportunities
  • Workplace advancement
  • Innovation ecosystems

Participants will discuss how equal access to opportunities can foster innovation and sustainable growth.

3. The Cost of Gender Gaps and Unlocking Gender Equality as an Economic Accelerator

This strand will focus on:

  • Economic consequences of gender inequality
  • Workforce participation gaps
  • Pay disparities
  • Productivity impacts
  • Strategies for promoting inclusive economic growth

Discussions will highlight how advancing gender equality benefits not only individuals but entire economies.

What to Expect at the Forum

The Gender Equality Forum 2026 will offer a diverse and engaging programme designed to encourage meaningful participation and collaboration.

Activities will include:

  • High-level debates
  • Interactive workshops
  • Expert panel discussions
  • Inspirational keynote talks
  • Networking opportunities
  • Collaborative problem-solving sessions

Participants will engage with:

  • Representatives of European Union institutions
  • Civil society organizations
  • Gender equality experts
  • Employers and business leaders
  • Researchers
  • Artists and cultural contributors
  • Fellow young changemakers

The event will be conducted entirely in English.

To enhance accessibility, selected sessions will include International Sign Language interpretation both onsite and online.

Benefits of Participating in the Gender Equality Forum 2026

Young participants selected for the Forum will enjoy a range of professional, personal, and networking benefits.

Financial Support

For up to 30 selected participants, EIGE will provide reimbursement for:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation costs
  • Daily allowances

This support helps remove financial barriers and promotes diverse participation from across the European Union.

Direct Access to High-Level Decision Makers

Participants will have opportunities to interact with:

  • EU policymakers
  • Senior institutional representatives
  • Gender equality specialists
  • Industry leaders
  • Progressive employers

This exposure can provide valuable insights into policymaking and advocacy.

Platform to Share Your Voice

Young attendees will be encouraged to actively contribute their ideas and perspectives throughout the event.

Participants may:

  • Ask questions during discussions
  • Share experiences and viewpoints
  • Contribute recommendations
  • Engage in collaborative solutions-building

Professional Networking Opportunities

The Forum creates a valuable environment for building relationships with:

  • Peers from across Europe
  • Experts in gender equality
  • Employers committed to inclusion
  • Civil society leaders

These connections can support future career and advocacy opportunities.

Active Participation in Forum Activities

Selected youth participants may also have opportunities to:

  • Appear in the Forum opening video
  • Be featured on EIGE’s website
  • Participate in question-and-answer sessions
  • Join thematic workshops
  • Contribute to discussions with EIGE leadership
  • Help document and promote the event through EIGE’s social media platforms

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Be between 18 and 30 years old at the time of application
  • Reside in one of the 27 European Union Member States
  • Non-EU nationals residing within the EU are also eligible
  • Have gained work experience through employment, traineeship, internship, or similar activities
  • Demonstrate commitment to gender equality as a core European value
  • Be motivated to actively participate in Forum discussions and activities
  • Be comfortable communicating in English
  • Be available to attend the event in person in Brussels, Belgium, on 17 November 2026

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The European Institute for Gender Equality strongly encourages applications from diverse backgrounds.

Applications are welcomed regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Racial background
  • Ethnic origin
  • Social background
  • Disability status
  • Religion or belief
  • Colour

This commitment reflects EIGE’s mission of promoting equality and inclusion throughout Europe.

Application Process

Interested candidates must complete and submit the official online application form provided by the European Institute for Gender Equality.

Applicants should ensure that they clearly demonstrate:

  • Their interest in gender equality
  • Relevant work or internship experience
  • Motivation for participation
  • Ability to contribute actively to Forum discussions

Application Deadline

Deadline: 7 July 2026 (End of Business Day)

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible to avoid last-minute issues.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Gender Equality Forum 2026 offers much more than attendance at a conference. It provides young people with a genuine opportunity to influence discussions surrounding the future of gender equality and economic development within the European Union.

By bringing together youth voices, policymakers, experts, employers, and civil society actors, the Forum creates a collaborative environment where innovative ideas can shape future policies and actions.

For young Europeans passionate about equality, inclusion, economic progress, and social change, this is a rare opportunity to engage directly with decision-makers while building valuable networks and gaining meaningful experience at the European level.

If you are ready to contribute your voice to one of Europe’s most important conversations on gender equality, now is the time to apply.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Conference Opportunities on OFY here.

The post European Institute for Gender Equality Gender Equality Forum 2026 Youth Participation Call: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young People Across the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and healthcare to governance, journalism, business, and communication. As AI systems become increasingly integrated into society, there is an urgent need for experts who can critically examine how these technologies influence humanity, ethics, public policy, culture, and everyday life. To help address these […]

The post Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming nearly every aspect of modern life—from education and healthcare to governance, journalism, business, and communication. As AI systems become increasingly integrated into society, there is an urgent need for experts who can critically examine how these technologies influence humanity, ethics, public policy, culture, and everyday life.

To help address these challenges, the Center for Humane Technology (CHT) has launched its inaugural Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship, a prestigious six-month remote fellowship designed to support interdisciplinary professionals who are committed to advancing thoughtful conversations and impactful research on AI and society.

The fellowship offers a $30,000 stipend, extensive mentorship, editorial support, media training, and an opportunity to become part of a growing community of experts shaping the future of responsible AI.

If you are a mid-career researcher, policy professional, journalist, advocate, technologist, or thought leader interested in AI’s societal impacts, this fellowship provides an outstanding opportunity to amplify your work while contributing to one of the world’s most important conversations.

Application Deadline: July 12, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST

About the Center for Humane Technology (CHT)

The Center for Humane Technology (CHT) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to ensuring that technology serves humanity’s best interests.

Rather than focusing solely on technological advancement, CHT works to understand how emerging technologies influence individuals, communities, institutions, and society as a whole. The organization aims to:

  • Clarify the societal impacts of rapidly evolving technologies.
  • Connect everyday technological harms to larger systemic issues.
  • Promote responsible technology development.
  • Encourage informed public conversations.
  • Support practical solutions that align innovation with human values.

Recognizing that these complex challenges require diverse perspectives, CHT established the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship to empower experts from multiple disciplines to contribute meaningful research, public engagement, and innovative ideas.

Fellowship Overview

The Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship is a six-month remote program that supports professionals exploring critical questions about AI’s impact on society.

Fellowship Details

  • Fellowship Type: Remote
  • Duration: September 14, 2026 – March 18, 2027
  • Commitment: 15 hours per week
  • Required overlap: At least 10 hours during Eastern Standard Time (EST)
  • Fellowship Cohort: 3 Fellows
  • Stipend: $30,000
  • Payment Structure: Paid upon completion of three program milestones

Fellowship Focus Areas

Applicants may apply to one or two of the following research areas.

1. AI and Cognition

This area examines how AI affects:

  • Human thinking
  • Learning
  • Decision-making
  • Critical thinking
  • Intellectual independence
  • Creativity
  • Cognitive development in adults and children
  • Diversity of thought
  • Human-machine collaboration

Researchers are encouraged to investigate whether AI merely enhances human capabilities or fundamentally changes the way people think.

2. AI and Relationships

This theme explores how artificial intelligence influences:

  • Human relationships
  • Social interaction
  • Communication
  • Emotional wellbeing
  • Community building
  • Human connection

3. AI and Surveillance

Applicants may investigate topics including:

  • Privacy
  • Data collection
  • Digital monitoring
  • Government surveillance
  • Corporate surveillance
  • Civil liberties
  • Human rights

4. AI and Spirituality

Potential research areas include:

  • Human meaning
  • Identity
  • Faith
  • Values
  • Purpose
  • Spiritual wellbeing
  • The philosophical implications of AI

5. Inside the Machine: AI from a Technologist’s Perspective

Ideal for professionals with technical backgrounds interested in explaining:

  • AI system development
  • Model design
  • Engineering decisions
  • Technical trade-offs
  • Industry practices
  • AI infrastructure
  • Responsible AI development

Fellowship Expectations

Selected fellows will actively contribute to CHT’s public engagement and research initiatives.

Each fellow will be expected to:

  • Complete one flagship research output such as:
    • Research paper
    • Long-form essay
    • Public report
  • Produce between one and three opinion articles (op-eds) for major publications or CHT’s Substack.
  • Participate in a podcast episode on Your Undivided Attention or another CHT platform where appropriate.
  • Contribute to CHT’s media and communications ecosystem.
  • Attend:
    • Three mandatory cohort meetings.
    • Mandatory bi-weekly meetings with the Senior Director of Strategy and Impact.

Fellowship Benefits

Participants receive far more than financial support.

Benefits include:

Financial Support

  • $30,000 stipend
  • Milestone-based payments

Professional Mentorship

  • Bi-weekly guidance from CHT’s Senior Director of Strategy and Impact
  • Research support from the CHT Research Team

Communications Support

  • Editorial assistance
  • Graphic design support
  • Communications support
  • Media training
  • Public relations assistance
  • Media placement opportunities

Professional Visibility

Fellows will also receive:

  • A CHT email account
  • Access to selected CHT Slack channels
  • Public profile on the CHT Fellows webpage
  • Opportunities to publish work reaching global audiences

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes professionals from interdisciplinary backgrounds, including but not limited to:

  • Researchers
  • Academics
  • Junior faculty members
  • Policy analysts
  • Former government officials
  • Journalists
  • Writers
  • Civil society advocates
  • Community organizers
  • Technology professionals
  • AI governance experts
  • Digital rights specialists
  • Platform accountability experts
  • Professionals studying technology’s social impacts

Relevant academic backgrounds may include:

  • Psychology
  • Sociology
  • Philosophy
  • Cognitive Science
  • Communications
  • Science and Technology Studies

Ideal Candidate Profile

Successful applicants should demonstrate:

Research Excellence

Applicants should have:

  • Strong analytical skills
  • Proven research experience
  • Ability to deliver high-quality work on schedule
  • Experience producing public-facing research

Communication Skills

Strong candidates should be able to:

  • Explain complex ideas clearly
  • Write for broad audiences
  • Speak publicly
  • Engage through social media or other communication platforms

An existing audience is helpful but not required.

Perspective on AI

Applicants should understand:

  • AI’s societal impacts
  • Technology governance
  • Digital rights
  • Policy development
  • Human-centered AI
  • The influence of business incentives on technology

They should also demonstrate:

  • Intellectual honesty
  • Critical thinking
  • Nuanced analysis
  • Commitment to practical impact

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. A 1–2 page research proposal for each selected topic (up to two topics).
  2. A CV or résumé (maximum two pages).
  3. A two-minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are a strong candidate.
  4. A writing sample demonstrating research and analytical ability.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship provides an exceptional platform for professionals seeking to influence one of today’s most important global conversations.

Participants will gain:

  • Financial support
  • National and international visibility
  • Professional mentorship
  • Media exposure
  • Editorial support
  • Opportunities to publish influential work
  • Networking with interdisciplinary experts
  • Experience contributing to responsible AI policy and public discourse

Whether your expertise lies in research, journalism, policymaking, technology, or advocacy, the Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to shape how artificial intelligence develops in ways that benefit humanity.

Important Dates

Activity Date
Applications Close July 12, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
Fellowship Begins September 14, 2026
Fellowship Ends March 18, 2027

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit all required application materials before the deadline through the official application portal.

Click here for more and to apply

For additional information about the fellowship and the Center for Humane Technology, visit the official fellowship page.

Click here for more global  Fellowship opportunities

The post Emerging Voices in AI and Society Fellowship 2026: Receive a $30,000 Stipend to Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence and Society (Remote Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026: Win a $10,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend, Expert Mentorship, Investor Access, and Climate Week NYC Participation
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from early-stage climate innovators around the world. Designed to bridge the critical gap between breakthrough research and market-ready solutions, this prestigious six-month hybrid fellowship equips researchers, scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and innovators with the tools, mentorship, funding, and industry connections needed to accelerate […]

The post New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026: Win a $10,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend, Expert Mentorship, Investor Access, and Climate Week NYC Participation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from early-stage climate innovators around the world. Designed to bridge the critical gap between breakthrough research and market-ready solutions, this prestigious six-month hybrid fellowship equips researchers, scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and innovators with the tools, mentorship, funding, and industry connections needed to accelerate climate technologies that strengthen energy systems and urban resilience.

Applications are now open and will be reviewed on a rolling basis until Saturday, August 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM.

Supported by the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA), the 2026 fellowship builds on the success of its inaugural 2025 cohort and focuses specifically on innovations at the intersection of Energy and Urban Resilience. Participants will receive structured commercialization support, a non-dilutive stipend, one-on-one mentoring, investor introductions, and the opportunity to participate in Climate Week NYC, one of the world’s premier climate events.

What Is the Climate Tech Fellowship?

The Climate Tech Fellowship is a six-month hybrid accelerator programme developed by The New York Climate Exchange to help promising climate technologies move beyond the research stage and become commercially viable solutions.

Many groundbreaking climate innovations never reach the marketplace because innovators often lack:

  • Commercialization expertise
  • Market strategy
  • Customer discovery experience
  • Investor connections
  • Mentorship
  • Venture-building support

This challenge is commonly referred to as the “first valley of death”—the difficult transition between research and successful commercialization.

The fellowship was created specifically to bridge this gap by helping innovators transform promising ideas into scalable climate solutions capable of creating real-world impact.

Mission of the Fellowship

The programme aims to accelerate climate innovation by supporting innovators who are developing technologies capable of addressing urgent global climate challenges.

Rather than offering funding alone, the fellowship combines financial support with structured business development, commercialization guidance, technical mentoring, and access to a powerful global innovation ecosystem.

Participants leave the programme with:

  • A clearer commercialization roadmap
  • Greater fundraising readiness
  • Stronger investor relationships
  • Expanded professional networks
  • Improved market positioning
  • Long-term mentorship

Focus Area for the 2026 Fellowship

The 2026 fellowship focuses exclusively on:

Energy & Urban Resilience

Cities face increasing risks from:

  • Extreme heat
  • Flooding
  • Climate-related disasters
  • Energy disruptions
  • Infrastructure failures

The fellowship seeks innovations that improve how cities prepare for, respond to, and recover from climate-related challenges while supporting the clean energy transition.

Innovations may focus on—but are not limited to—the following areas:

1. End-use Energy Innovation

Examples include:

  • Intelligent buildings
  • Building energy efficiency
  • Smart infrastructure
  • Energy management systems

2. Power Generation & Storage

Projects involving:

  • Renewable generation
  • Battery technologies
  • Distributed energy systems
  • Energy storage innovations

3. Grid Modernization

Solutions addressing:

  • Smart grids
  • Power system monitoring
  • Grid communications
  • Adaptive electricity management
  • Grid resilience

4. Natural Carbon Solutions

Innovations related to:

  • Green infrastructure
  • Carbon storage
  • Nature-based solutions
  • Sustainable land management

Cross-cutting innovations spanning multiple focus areas are also encouraged. Applicants uncertain about their project’s fit are encouraged to apply or contact the programme team.

What Fellows Receive

Selected fellows gain access to an extensive package of financial, technical, and professional support.

$10,000 Non-Dilutive Funding

Each selected team receives:

  • A $10,000 non-dilutive stipend

Importantly, participants do not give up equity in exchange for funding.

Commercialization Curriculum

Participants receive structured training covering:

  • Market mapping
  • Customer segmentation
  • Commercialization strategy
  • Go-to-market planning
  • Target market identification
  • Sales channels
  • Venture development

Expert Sessions

The fellowship also includes specialized sessions on:

  • Fundraising
  • Intellectual property (IP)
  • Climate equity
  • Communications strategy

Personalized Mentorship

Every fellow receives:

  • Dedicated one-on-one mentoring
  • Mentors matched to the fellow’s technical area
  • Guidance aligned with stage of development

Customized Fellowship Blueprint

Participants receive:

  • Individualized commercialization roadmap
  • Monthly progress check-ins
  • Tailored milestone planning

Climate Week NYC Participation

One of the fellowship highlights is participation in Climate Week NYC.

Selected fellows receive:

  • In-person programming
  • Investor showcase opportunities
  • Partner engagement
  • Networking sessions

Travel and accommodation for one team member are fully covered.

Global Network Access

Participants also benefit from introductions to:

  • Investors
  • Policymakers
  • Strategic partners
  • Climate organizations
  • Innovation ecosystem leaders

Programme Timeline

The fellowship runs from September 2026 to February 2027.

September 2026

  • Programme onboarding
  • Climate Week NYC participation

October 2026 – January 2027

Virtual programming including:

  • Commercialization modules
  • Mentor meetings
  • Cohort sessions
  • Customer discovery
  • Venture development

February 2027

  • Virtual showcase
  • Programme wrap-up

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is intended for early-stage innovators developing technologies that strengthen energy systems and urban resilience.

Ideal applicants include:

  • Researchers
  • Scientists
  • Engineers
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Startup founders

Applicants should ideally be:

  • At prototype stage
  • At early pilot stage
  • At early commercialization stage

The fellowship is particularly valuable for innovators seeking commercialization support rather than research funding alone.

University affiliation is not required, and applicants do not need to have incorporated a company. The programme is designed for innovations from the pre-company formation to pre-seed stage.

Applications from founders from underrepresented backgrounds are especially encouraged.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should meet the following criteria:

  • Be an early-stage innovator, researcher, scientist, engineer, founder, or entrepreneur.
  • Be developing an innovation related to Energy and Urban Resilience.
  • Have a prototype, early pilot, or early commercialization-stage solution.
  • Be interested in commercialization, customer discovery, and venture building.
  • Commit several hours per week throughout the six-month programme.
  • Be available for virtual sessions and in-person participation during Climate Week NYC.
  • Be willing to focus on one innovation throughout the fellowship.

Notably:

  • University affiliation is not required.
  • Company incorporation is not required.
  • Teams may apply.
  • Applicants from any country are eligible.

Application Process

Applications are completed online and are free of charge.

Applicants should submit details about:

  • Their innovation
  • Team members
  • Fellowship goals
  • Commercialization plans

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

The review process includes:

  1. Eligibility screening
  2. Completeness check
  3. Evaluation of commercialization readiness
  4. Assessment of impact potential
  5. Alignment with the 2026 focus area
  6. Virtual interviews for shortlisted applicants
  7. Final selection and onboarding

APPLY HERE

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Is the fellowship free?

Yes. There is no application fee and participation is free for selected fellows.

Is the fellowship open internationally?

Yes. The programme is open to innovators globally.

Do I need to live in New York?

No. The fellowship is primarily virtual. Fellows only travel to New York during Climate Week NYC.

Do I need to be affiliated with a university?

No. Independent innovators are welcome to apply.

Do I need to have a registered company?

No. Incorporation is not required. The fellowship specifically supports innovators before company formation or at the pre-seed stage.

Can teams apply?

Yes. Teams are welcome. However, each team receives one $10,000 stipend, and travel support is provided for one team member.

Can I apply if my innovation is still in research?

Yes. The programme supports research-stage innovations provided applicants are committed to commercialization.

Can I apply with multiple projects?

Yes, but applicants should focus on one specific innovation during the fellowship.

How many fellows will be selected?

The Exchange expects to select between five and ten teams for the 2026 cohort.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Climate innovation often stalls before reaching the market due to limited access to commercialization expertise, strategic partnerships, and investment opportunities. The Climate Tech Fellowship directly addresses these barriers by combining financial support with mentorship, expert guidance, investor exposure, and structured venture development.

For innovators working at the intersection of clean energy and urban resilience, the programme provides an exceptional platform to refine technologies, strengthen commercialization strategies, and build lasting relationships within the global climate innovation ecosystem.

Visit HERE to learn more about the New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026.

Conclusion

The New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for early-stage climate innovators worldwide to transform promising ideas into commercially viable solutions. Through a six-month hybrid programme, fellows receive a $10,000 non-dilutive stipend, personalized mentorship, commercialization training, investor introductions, and the opportunity to showcase their innovations during Climate Week NYC.

Whether you are a researcher, engineer, scientist, entrepreneur, or founder developing solutions in energy, grid modernization, power storage, natural carbon solutions, or urban resilience, this fellowship offers the resources and network needed to accelerate your innovation toward real-world impact.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, but the final deadline is Saturday, August 1, 2026, at 11:59 PM. Early applications are encouraged to maximize your chances of selection.

Discover more global Fellowship, Tech, and Climate Action opportunities on OFY.

The post New York Climate Exchange Climate Tech Fellowship 2026: Win a $10,000 Non-Dilutive Stipend, Expert Mentorship, Investor Access, and Climate Week NYC Participation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The CIVICUS Global Alliance is offering multiple high-impact opportunities in 2026 for researchers, consultants, evaluators, and interns interested in civic space, human rights monitoring, data analysis, and global civil society strengthening. These roles support the CIVICUS Monitor, one of the world’s leading platforms tracking civic freedoms across nearly 200 countries and territories. These openings are […]

The post CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CIVICUS Global Alliance is offering multiple high-impact opportunities in 2026 for researchers, consultants, evaluators, and interns interested in civic space, human rights monitoring, data analysis, and global civil society strengthening. These roles support the CIVICUS Monitor, one of the world’s leading platforms tracking civic freedoms across nearly 200 countries and territories.

These openings are ideal for candidates passionate about democracy, human rights, international relations, political science, data coding, and global development. They also contribute directly to global reports such as the People Power Under Attack annual publication and other civic space monitoring initiatives.

 

About CIVICUS Global Alliance

CIVICUS is a global alliance of civil society organizations working in over 180 countries. Its mission is to strengthen citizen participation, defend civic freedoms, and amplify voices from the Global South.

Through its CIVICUS Monitor, the organization documents violations of freedoms of association, peaceful assembly, and expression while producing global civic space ratings and analytical reports used by policymakers, researchers, and civil society actors worldwide.

 

1. Civic Space Research Intern (2026)

Overview of the Role

The Civic Space Research Intern will support the CIVICUS Monitor team in strengthening global research collaboration ahead of the People Power Under Attack report. The intern will contribute to:

  • Research on civic space incidents globally
  • Implementation of the CIVICUS Monitor incident tracking tool
  • Data entry and analysis of civic space violations
  • Administrative and coordination support tasks
  • Contribution to annual global reporting processes

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree (completed or near completion) in international relations, political science, or related field
  • Strong written and spoken English
  • Knowledge or experience in human rights research
  • Understanding of international human rights frameworks (association, expression, peaceful assembly)
  • Additional UN language proficiency is an advantage

Application Requirements

  • CV
  • One-page motivation letter
  • Applications must be original (AI-generated applications are not allowed)

Deadline

8 July 2026, 11:59 PM SAST

Start Date

First week of August 2026

 

2. Data Coding Consultant – CIVICUS Monitor & EU SEE Programme

Overview of the Role

This consultancy focuses on coding and structuring civic space data for analysis and reporting. Consultants will:

  • Code civic space incidents from published CIVICUS Monitor updates
  • Enter data into standardized civic space incident tools
  • Support EU System for an Enabling Environment (EU SEE) data analysis
  • Transform qualitative reports into structured datasets for trend analysis
  • Contribute to early warning and civic space monitoring systems

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or current student in international relations, political science, social sciences, or related fields
  • Strong Excel and data analysis skills
  • Experience in human rights research or monitoring preferred
  • Strong English communication skills
  • Ability to work independently and remotely
  • High attention to detail and deadlines

Application Requirements

  • CV
  • Cover letter detailing coding/data experience
  • Proposed hourly and daily rates (USD or ZAR depending on location)
  • Availability per week

Deadline

8 July 2026

 

3. Civic Space Research Consultants (Regional Experts)

Overview of the Role

This consultancy supports drafting and editing CIVICUS Monitor country updates and briefs. Consultants will:

  • Write and edit civic space country reports
  • Analyze trends in human rights and civic freedoms
  • Support monitoring of association, expression, and assembly rights
  • Provide regional expertise (Africa, Asia, Latin America, Middle East, etc.)
  • Contribute to ongoing global civic space documentation

Requirements

  • Master’s degree preferred (Political Science, Law, Sociology, International Relations, Journalism, etc.)
  • Minimum 3 years of experience with civil society or human rights organizations
  • Strong understanding of global civic space trends
  • Regional language skills (French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, etc. depending on region)
  • Strong writing and analytical skills in English
  • Ability to work independently

Application Requirements

  • CV with writing samples
  • Motivation letter specifying regional expertise
  • Budget (hourly/daily rates in USD or ZAR)

Deadline

8 July 2026

 

4. External End-of-Phase Evaluator (Netherlands MFA Partnership 2021–2026)

Overview of the Role

CIVICUS is seeking an independent evaluator or evaluation team to assess its strategic partnership with the Netherlands Ministry of Foreign Affairs under the Power of Voices framework.

The evaluation covers 2021–2026 and includes:

  • CIVICUS Monitor
  • Membership and networks
  • Advocacy campaigns (#StandAsMyWitness)
  • Solidarity actions and global initiatives
  • International Civil Society Week

Key Responsibilities

  • Inception phase design and evaluation framework development
  • Mixed-methods data collection (interviews, surveys, case studies)
  • Outcome harvesting and contribution analysis
  • Global stakeholder engagement (especially Global South actors)
  • Final evaluation report (max 60 pages + annexes)

Requirements

  • Experience evaluating international civil society organizations
  • Knowledge of OECD/DAC or IOB evaluation criteria
  • Expertise in qualitative evaluation methods
  • Strong English communication skills (additional languages an advantage)
  • Independence from CIVICUS-funded programs

Deliverables Timeline

  • Inception report: August 2026
  • Interim briefing: October 2026
  • Draft report: November 2026
  • Final report: January 2027

Deadline

13 July 2026, 23:59 SAST

 

General Notes for All Opportunities

  • All roles are remote or partially remote
  • Strong focus on civic space, human rights, and civil society strengthening
  • Preference for candidates with Global South representation
  • High emphasis on independent, ethical, and non-AI-generated application materials (where applicable)
  • Selected candidates may join CIVICUS preferred supplier lists for up to two years

 

  • More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post CIVICUS Global Alliance 2026 Opportunities: Internships, Research Roles, Data Coding & Evaluation Consultancies in Civic Space Monitoring (Apply by July 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026 is now accepting applications for one of the organization’s newest global talent initiatives designed to cultivate the next generation of development professionals. This prestigious two-year international programme offers young, high-potential professionals from eligible donor and developed countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on real-world development […]

The post World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Global Career Opportunity for Young Professionals from Donor Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Bank Group (WBG) Explorers Program 2026 is now accepting applications for one of the organization’s newest global talent initiatives designed to cultivate the next generation of development professionals. This prestigious two-year international programme offers young, high-potential professionals from eligible donor and developed countries the opportunity to gain hands-on experience working on real-world development projects across World Bank Group country offices around the globe.

Through an immersive, professionally supported experience, participants will contribute directly to development initiatives while expanding their global perspective, strengthening technical skills, and building careers dedicated to addressing some of the world’s most pressing economic, environmental, and social challenges.

Applications for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) donor cycle are currently open and will close on 15 July 2026.

This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the WBG Explorers Program 2026, including its objectives, programme features, eligibility requirements, available assignments, benefits, application process, and why it represents an outstanding career opportunity for aspiring international development professionals.

What Is the WBG Explorers Program?

The World Bank Group Explorers Program is an international talent programme created by the World Bank Group to connect outstanding young professionals from donor and developed countries with the organization’s mission of ending extreme poverty and promoting shared prosperity.

The programme enables participants to gain direct exposure to World Bank Group operations by working on development projects across various sectors while living and working in WBG Country Offices around the world.

Beyond professional development, the programme seeks to develop future leaders who will become lifelong advocates for international development and the World Bank Group’s mission.

Participants contribute meaningfully to projects while gaining valuable international experience that strengthens both technical expertise and leadership capacity.

Objectives of the WBG Explorers Program

The programme has several key objectives:

  • Connect talented young professionals with the World Bank Group’s development mission.
  • Build future global development leaders.
  • Provide practical experience working on international development projects.
  • Strengthen understanding of global development challenges.
  • Create ambassadors who will continue supporting sustainable development throughout their careers.
  • Foster cross-cultural collaboration and international professional networks.

Programme Features

The WBG Explorers Program has been carefully designed to provide a structured and highly practical learning experience.

Participants receive:

Two-Year Extended Term Contract (ETC)

Successful applicants receive a two-year Extended Term Contract (ETC) with the World Bank Group.

The contract allows participants to gain substantial professional experience rather than short-term exposure, enabling them to make meaningful contributions to development operations.

Country Office Placement

Unlike many international programmes that are based at headquarters, WBG Explorers are placed directly within World Bank Group Country Offices around the world.
